revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sanni_Ryo]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

散位寮（さんにりょう）は古代日本の律令制において[[式部省]]に属する機関の一つである。
== 職掌 == 散位寮は散位を名簿によって管理しその考課を行う。散位（さんに）は散官ともいい位階のみで官職を持たないもののことをいう。[[律令制期の官吏制度|京官]]・[[律令制期の官吏制度|外官]]および[[律令制期の官吏制度|文官]]・[[律令制期の官吏制度|武官]]の別がある。散位の大半は退職者である。五位以上の散位は散位寮に長上（常勤）し､六位以下のうち､在京のものは散位寮に分番（交替勤務）し､他は[[国府]]に分番して､他の官司の手伝いや使者としての事務を行う。また武散位（武官）は[[兵部省]]の管理をも受ける。その他[[四度使]]（よどのつかい）の一つである[[朝集使]]が在京中のときはその管理を行う。のちに散位制度が形骸化すると存在意義が薄れ寛平八年（896年）式部省に併合され､以後散位の事務も式部省で行われた。
== 職員 == *頭（従五位下相当）一名 *助（従六位上相当）一名 *允（従七位下相当）一名 *大属（従八位下相当）一名 少属（大初位上相当）一名
*史生 六名のち四名
*散位
*使部 *直丁
== 関連項目 == *[[律令制期の官吏制度]]
[[category:日本の律令制|かんせい]] [[category:律令制の官制|*]] [[category:廃止された日本の国家機関|*]] [[Category:日本の制度史|かんせい]] {{History-stub}}

== 職員 == *頭（従五位下相当）一名 *助（従六位上相当）一名 *允（従七位下相当）一名 *大属（従八位下相当）一名 少属（大初位上相当）一名

'''散位寮'''（さんにりょう）は古代[[日本]]の[[律令制]]において[[式部省]]に属する機関の一つである。
== 職掌 == 散位寮は[[散位]]を名簿によって管理しその考課を行う。[[官人|散位]]（さんに）は散官ともいい位階のみで官職を持たないもののことをいう。[[京官]]・[[外官]]および[[文官]]・[[武官]]の別がある。散位の大半は退職者である。五位以上の散位は散位寮に[[長上]]（常勤）し､六位以下のうち､在京のものは散位寮に[[分番]]（交替勤務）し､他は[[国府]]に分番して､他の[[官司]]の手伝いや使者としての事務を行う。また武散位（武官の散位）は[[兵部省]]の管理をも受ける。その他[[四度使]]（よどのつかい）の一つである[[朝集使]]が在京中のときはその管理を行う。のちに散位制度が形骸化すると存在意義が薄れ[[寛平]]八年（[[896年]]）[[式部省]]に併合され､以後散位の事務も[[式部省]]で行われた。
== 職員 == *頭（[[従五位下]]相当）一名 *助（[[従六位#従六位|従六位上]]相当）一名 *允（[[従六位#従七位|従七位下]]相当）一名 *大属（[[従六位#従八位|従八位下]]相当）一名 少属（[[従六位#大初位|大初位上]]相当）一名 *[[官人#雑任|史生]] 六名のち四名 *散位 *使部 *[[官人#仕丁|仕丁]]
== 関連項目 == *[[官人]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんにりよう}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub|さんにりよう}}

== 職掌 == 散位寮は[[散位]]を名簿によって管理しその考課を行う。
[[官人|散位]]（さんに）は散官ともいい[[位階]]のみで[[官職]]を持たないもののことをいう。[[京官]]・[[外官]]および[[文官]]・[[武官]]の別がある。散位の大半は退職者である。五位以上の散位は散位寮に[[長上]]（常勤）し､六位以下のうち､在京のものは散位寮に[[分番]]（交替勤務）し､他は[[国府]]に分番して､他の[[官司]]の手伝いや使者としての事務を行う。また武散位（武官の散位）は[[兵部省]]の管理をも受ける。
その他[[四度使]]（よどのつかい）の一つである[[朝集使]]が在京中のときはその管理を行う。のちに散位制度が形骸化すると存在意義が薄れ[[寛平]]8年（[[896年]]）[[式部省]]に併合され､以後散位の事務も[[式部省]]で行われた。

revision.text:
'''散位寮'''（さんいりょう/さんにりょう）は古代[[日本]]の[[律令制]]において[[式部省]]に属する機関の一つである。なお、唐の制度には散位寮に該当する機関は存在しない。
== 職掌 == 散位寮は[[散位]]を名簿によって管理しその考課を行う。
[[散位]]（さんい/さんに）は散官ともいい[[位階]]のみで[[官職]]を持たないもののことをいう。[[京官]]・[[外官]]および[[文官]]・[[武官]]の別がある。散位の大半は退職者である。五位以上の散位は散位寮に[[長上]]（常勤）し、六位以下のうち、在京のものは散位寮に[[分番]]（交替勤務）し、他は[[国府]]に分番して、他の[[官司]]の手伝いや使者としての事務を行う。また武散位（武官の散位）は[[兵部省]]の管理をも受ける。
その他[[四度使]]（よどのつかい）の一つである[[朝集使]]が在京中のときはその管理を行う。のちに散位制度が形骸化すると存在意義が薄れ[[寛平]]8年（[[896年]]）[[式部省]]に併合され、以後散位の事務も式部省で行われた。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system. == Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service. San'i, also known as "scattered officials"..."

revision.text:
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court rank|ja|位階}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|ja|国府}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}.
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head (Tō)|ja|頭}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁}}== See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人}}

San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court rank|ja|位階}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職}}. Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|ja|国府}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}.
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head (Tō)|ja|頭}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁}}== See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人}}

Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|ja|国府}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}.
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head (Tō)|ja|頭}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁}} == See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人}}

revision.text:
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|ja|式部省}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court rank|ja|位階}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|ja|国府}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}.
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head (Tō)|ja|頭}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属}} (equivalent to {{ill|Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁}} == See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人}}
<!--Sanni Ryo-->
<!--Sanni Ryo-->
<!--Sanni Ryo--> {{Japanese-history-stub|San'iryō}}

revision.text:
'''散位寮'''（さんいりょう/さんにりょう）は古代[[日本]]の[[律令制]]において[[式部省]]に属する機関の一つである。なお、唐の制度には散位寮に該当する機関は存在しない。
== 職掌 == 散位寮は[[散位]]を名簿によって管理しその考課を行う。
[[散位]]（さんい/さんに）は散官ともいい[[位階]]のみで[[官職]]を持たないもののことをいう。[[京官]]・[[外官]]および[[文官]]・[[武官]]の別がある。散位の大半は退職者である。五位以上の散位は散位寮に[[長上]]（常勤）し、六位以下のうち、在京のものは散位寮に[[分番]]（交替勤務）し、他は[[国府]]に分番して、他の[[官司]]の手伝いや使者としての事務を行う。また武散位（武官の散位）は[[兵部省]]の管理をも受ける。
その他[[四度使]]（よどのつかい）の一つである[[朝集使]]が在京中のときはその管理を行う。のちに散位制度が形骸化すると存在意義が薄れ[[寛平]]8年（[[896年]]）[[式部省]]に併合され、以後散位の事務も式部省で行われた。
== 職員 == * 頭（[[従五位|従五位下]]相当）一名 * 助（[[従六位|従六位上]]相当）一名 * 允（[[従七位|従七位下]]相当）一名 * 大属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当）一名 少属（[[大初位|大初位上]]相当）一名 * [[史生]] 六名のち四名 * [[散位]] * 使部 * [[官人#仕丁|仕丁]]
== 関連項目 == * [[官人]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんいりよう}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub|さんいりよう}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[散位寮]] to [[Sanni Ryo]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''散位寮'''（さんいりょう/さんにりょう）は古代[[日本]]の[[律令制]]において[[式部省]]に属する機関の一つである。なお、唐の制度には散位寮に該当する機関は存在しない。
== 職掌 == 散位寮は[[散位]]を名簿によって管理しその考課を行う。
[[散位]]（さんい/さんに）は散官ともいい[[位階]]のみで[[官職]]を持たないもののことをいう。[[京官]]・[[外官]]および[[文官]]・[[武官]]の別がある。散位の大半は退職者である。五位以上の散位は散位寮に[[長上]]（常勤）し、六位以下のうち、在京のものは散位寮に[[分番]]（交替勤務）し、他は[[国府]]に分番して、他の[[官司]]の手伝いや使者としての事務を行う。また武散位（武官の散位）は[[兵部省]]の管理をも受ける。
その他[[四度使]]（よどのつかい）の一つである[[朝集使]]が在京中のときはその管理を行う。のちに散位制度が形骸化すると存在意義が薄れ[[寛平]]8年（[[896年]]）[[式部省]]に併合され、以後散位の事務も式部省で行われた。
== 職員 == * 頭（[[従五位|従五位下]]相当）一名 * 助（[[従六位|従六位上]]相当）一名 * 允（[[従七位|従七位下]]相当）一名 * 大属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当）一名 少属（[[大初位|大初位上]]相当）一名 * [[史生]] 六名のち四名 * [[散位]] * 使部 * [[官人#仕丁|仕丁]]
== 関連項目 == * [[官人]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんいりよう}} [[category:律令制の官制]] {{Japanese-history-stub|さんいりよう}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11499496]]
Wikidata name: Saniryo
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.

== Responsibilities ==
The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.

San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|qq=|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|qq=|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|qq=|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|qq=|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees. 

San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|qq=|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].

Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|qq=|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|qq=|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4
* {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}}
* {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichō|qq=|1=Shichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361855}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人|1=kanjin|2=ja|3=官人|4=ko|5=관인|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Officials (kanjin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}

'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.

The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.

These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].

Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]]
Wikidata name: Saniryo
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.

== Responsibilities ==
The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.

San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees. 

San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].

Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position
* {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4
* {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}}
* {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichō|1=Shichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361855}}
== See Also ==

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.

== Responsibilities ==
The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.

San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees. 

San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].

Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]] Wikidata name: Saniryo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichō|1=Shichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361855}} == See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人|1=kanjin|2=ja|3=官人|4=ko|5=관인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Officials (kanjin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}

Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]] Wikidata name: Saniryo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichō|1=Shichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361855}} == See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人|1=kanjin|2=ja|3=官人|4=ko|5=관인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Officials (kanjin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}

Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]] Wikidata name: Saniryo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichō|1=Shichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361855}} == See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人|1=kanjin|2=ja|3=官人|4=ko|5=관인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Officials (kanjin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]] Wikidata name: Saniryo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]] Wikidata name: Saniryo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] in the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ([[:en:Kokufu|Kokufu]]). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]].
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * [[:en:Head|Head (Tō)]] (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position * revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499496]] Wikidata name: Saniryo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|WD=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Kokufu}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}.
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head|en|Head|WD=Q23640|lt=Head (Tō)}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shichō|ja|仕丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichō|1=Shichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361855}} == See Also == * {{ill|Officials (kanjin)|ja|官人|1=kanjin|2=ja|3=官人|4=ko|5=관인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Officials (kanjin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452075}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]]
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|WD=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Chōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Kokufu}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}.

As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head|en|Head|WD=Q23640|lt=Head (Tō)|1=Head}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位寮]][[zh:散位寮]]
'''San'iryō''' (散位寮) was one of the institutions under the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|WD=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs|1=Shikibu-shō}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Notably, there was no equivalent institution to San'iryō in the Tang dynasty system.
== Responsibilities == The San'iryō managed the {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} (unassigned officials) through a roster system and conducted evaluations of their service.
San'i, also known as "scattered officials" or "scattered Ranks," referred to individuals who held a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} but did not occupy an official {{ill|government post|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government post|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452125}}. San'i included divisions of {{ill|Kyōkan|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōkan|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} (metropolitan officials) and {{ill|Gekan|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekan|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}} (provincial officials) as well as {{ill|monkan|ja|文官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monkan|1=civilian official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16919908}} (civil officials) and {{ill|bukan|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bukan|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}} (military officials). Most San'i were retirees.
San'i of the fifth Rank or higher worked full-time ({{ill|Chōjō|ja|長上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjō|1=Q11651393|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651393}}) at the San'iryō. Those of the sixth Rank and below who lived in the capital rotated duties ({{ill|Bunban|ja|分番|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) at the San'iryō, while others performed similar duties at provincial offices ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Kokufu|1=Kokufu}}). These duties included assisting other government offices and acting as messengers. Military San'i (武散位), on the other hand, were also managed by the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}.
Additionally, the San'iryō managed the {{ill|Chōshūshi|ja|朝集使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūshi|1=chōshūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517747}}, one of the {{ill|Shido no tsukai|ja|四度使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido no tsukai|qq=}} (four types of messengers), while they were stationed in the capital. As the San'i system became increasingly symbolic, the institution's importance declined, leading to its merger with the Shikibu-shō in {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 8 (896). Thereafter, the Shikibu-shō took over the management of San'i affairs.
== Staff == * {{ill|Head|en|Head|WD=Q23640|lt=Head (Tō)|1=Head}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Assistant (Suke)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (Suke)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Secretary (Yō)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secretary (Yō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja|大属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (Daizoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja|少属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (Shōzoku)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (equivalent to {{ill|Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}) – 1 position * {{ill|Shishō|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishō|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}} – Initially 6 positions, later reduced to 4 * {{ill|San'i|ja|散位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i|qq=}} * {{ill|Shibu (servants)|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu (servants)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
